title: Kelp cold-process soap
subtitle: A sea-mineral bar made with kelp powder and sea salt, on an olive, coconut, and castor base
excerpt: Kelp powder gives this cold-process bar a muted olive-green colour and a soft mineral character. A small addition of sea salt in the lye water produces a slightly firmer, smoother bar. The oil blend of olive, coconut, and castor makes a lathering, conditioning bar suited to normal and combination skin.
sourceNotes: SAP values: olive oil 0.134, coconut oil 0.190, castor oil 0.128. 5% superfat; 33% lye discount. Reference: Susan Miller Cavitch, The Soapmaker's Companion (1997). Kelp powder as a soap additive: Bramble Berry natural ingredients guide (2023). Sea salt in soap lye water: salt increases hardness and produces a smoother bar surface; use at low percentages (under 2% of water weight) to avoid problems with trace speed. Kelp (Ascophyllum nodosum / Fucus spp.): dried and powdered for cosmetic use.

recipe.batchNotes: Recalculate lye weight for any oil changes. Kelp and sea salt are additives; they do not affect the lye calculation.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Allow 2 days in the mould. Minimum 4-week cure; 6 weeks for a harder bar.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Fully cured bars keep two years on an open rack in a cool, dark place. Discard on signs of DOS (orange spots) or a paint-like smell.

glossaryTerms.definition: The point at which the soap batter has thickened to a custard-like consistency. The signal that oils and lye solution have emulsified.
The percentage of oil left unsaponified in the finished bar. At 5%, the bar is conditioning and mild.
The reaction between fat and lye that produces soap and glycerine. SAP values vary by oil; the lye weight is calculated for this specific blend.

projectSchedule.title: Unmould and cut
projectSchedule.body: Unmould after 2 days. The bar will be a muted olive-green. Cut wearing protective gloves.

projectSchedule.title: Four-week check
projectSchedule.body: Test a sliver on skin. Ready to use.

projectSchedule.title: Cure complete
projectSchedule.body: Fully cured. Hard, mild, stable for two years.

content.text: Kelp powder dispersed in the soap batter at

gives this bar a muted olive-green colour and a mineral character. A small addition of fine sea salt dissolved in the lye water produces a slightly firmer bar with a smoother surface. The oil base of 65% olive, 30% coconut, and 5% castor gives conditioning and lather. At 5%
and 33% lye discount, the bar is mild for daily use. Yield is 8 bars of around 120 g each.
Put on protective gloves and goggles before starting.

items.prepNote: 30% of oil weight; melt if solid
33% of oil weight; sea salt is dissolved in this
Fine sea salt; dissolved in the distilled water before adding lye
Cosmetic or food-grade; dispersed in 1 tablespoon of oil before addition at trace

content.text: Mix the kelp powder into 1 tablespoon of the weighed olive oil until smooth. Set aside.

In a ventilated space with goggles and protective gloves on, dissolve the sea salt in the measured distilled water. Weigh the lye separately. Add lye to the salt water in a steady stream, stirring continuously. Never reverse. The solution heats to around 80°C; stand back and ventilate. Cool to 38 to 42°C.

Combine all oils in a heat-safe bowl. Cool to 38 to 42°C. Pour the lye solution into the oils. Stick-blend in short bursts to light trace. Stir in the kelp-oil mixture and blend for 5 seconds. Pour into the mould. Wrap in a towel and leave for 2 days. The
Unmould and cut into bars wearing protective gloves. Cure on an open rack for 4 to 6 weeks in a cool, dry room, turning every two weeks.
Two years on an open rack in a cool, dark place. Discard on signs of DOS or a paint-like smell.
